plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad market exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to diversify and manage single-name risk.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall book size.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and low drawdown.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month return, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all below-stop and leveraged ETF holdings.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it is flagged for trimming.
Buy CRM and META, allocating 10% to each, leveraging top scanner setups.
This approach balances broad market exposure with quality tech and reduced leverage.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month growth with a risk-first approach. By reducing leverage and diversifying into high-quality tech, it seeks to improve risk-adjusted returns while managing drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to concentration limits.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit all below-stop and underperforming leveraged ETFs.
Trim QQQI to 10% as it is flagged for trimming, reallocating capital.
Buy CRM and META, allocating 15% to each, concentrating in high-scoring setups.
Buy TECS, a leveraged ETF, allocating 5% for aggressive growth potential.
This plan uses leverage and concentration to press what is working, with strict stops.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth by leaning into leverage and concentration. It carries significantly higher risk and potential for larger drawdowns, especially with leveraged ETFs, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad index concentration.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta sector and single-name exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to park cash for yield.
income.read: This income plan aims for ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While offering cash-like income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs, so it's not a savings account.
